title: Global price observation/history scope
description: Confirm and document that product_price_observations, product_new_price_history, and product_price_candidates require no code changes to become correctly global once product_id refers to a shared product — record_price_observation() and _recompute_used_price() are already pure product_id-keyed functions with no item_id dependency.
acceptance_criteria: record_price_observation, _recompute_used_price, record_new_price_history, record_price_candidates are reviewed and confirmed to need zero code changes for global scope (or any needed change is identified and made here, not assumed)
A test demonstrates two different projects' matched listings for the same global product both contribute to one shared typical_used_price median
price_trend.py's trend calculation is confirmed to operate correctly over the combined, larger observation set with no change needed
No new column or table is introduced by this feature — it is a verification/documentation feature confirming FEATURE-1000/1001's design has no hidden price-history coupling to item_id
notes: See docs/adr/0007-catalogue-globalization.md, 'How price history should be treated'. This feature exists to make explicit and test what the ADR asserts is already true, rather than leave it as an unverified claim.
